claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

**First, let me ask about the child (or children) you're claiming for:**

How old is the child you're seeking Child Benefit for? (If you have multiple children, let's start with the first one and their name)

The age determines the initial eligibility pathway:
- **Under 16**: Generally automatically qualifies
- **16-19**: May qualify if in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me:
1. The child's name
2. The child's age (or date of birth)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through Alex's eligibility.

Since Alex is 9 years old, they fall into the **under 16** category. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news so far! Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is 9 years old, I assume they're not in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all - they're only 9, so they're just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's what I expected. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

Specifically, this means Alex is not receiving:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA), or
- Universal Credit

...in their own right (as the claimant themselves).

For a 9-year-old, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - is Alex receiving any benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name - they're just a child living at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 9 years old and in school, I assume they're not in an apprenticeship program, but I need to confirm. Apprenticeships can affect eligibility for older children (typically 16+), depending on the type and region.

Is Alex currently in any apprenticeship program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - Alex is only 9 and just in primary school, so no apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check your residency status. This is an important requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **ANY** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status AND:
   - You're working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months), OR
   - You're actively seeking work (for up to 91 days), OR
   - You have sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and not claiming certain benefits), OR
   - You're studying with sufficient resources, OR
   - You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

**Which of these applies to you?** The most straightforward is if you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That meets the residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now let's check responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You meet this requirement if **ANY** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex** (most common situation)
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (or equivalent in kind) - this includes food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
3. You pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement that equals or exceeds the Child Benefit amount

Based on what you've told me (Alex lives at home with you), it sounds like you live with Alex. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now an important question about other claimants:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. This is important to check because:

- If no one else is claiming, we can proceed
- If someone else is claiming but you've both agreed that you should claim instead, we can proceed
- If there's a dispute about who should claim, HMRC will need to make the decision

So, is anyone else (such as Alex's other parent or another person) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check Alex's living situation, as there are special rules for certain circumstances.

**Which of the following applies to Alex?**

1. **Normal living situation** - Alex lives at home with you (most common)
2. **In hospital** - Alex is currently in hospital
3. **In care** - Alex is in local authority care
4. **Fostered** - Alex is fostered

Based on what you've told me, it sounds like Alex is living normally at home with you in Manchester. Is that correct, or is Alex in any of these special situations (hospital, care, or fostered)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex just lives at home with me in a normal situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the local council paying for Alex's care or living costs? 

Since Alex lives at home with you in a normal situation, I expect the answer is that the council is NOT paying, but I need to confirm this to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I'm just raising Alex myself at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me navigate to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE**

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit for Alex. Based on our assessment:

✅ Alex is 9 years old (under 16 - automatically qualifies)  
✅ Alex is not in paid work  
✅ Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right  
✅ Alex is not in an apprenticeship  
✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)  
✅ You live with and are responsible for Alex  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ Alex lives at home in a normal situation  
✅ The local council is not paying for Alex's care  

---

## Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge (but you can still claim)
5. **Payments**: Made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

**Ready to apply?** Visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16 - automatic qualification age; (2) Alex is not in paid work; (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives at home in a normal situation; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their 9-year-old child Alex. The child qualifies as under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and none of the disqualifying factors apply.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their 9-year-old child Alex. The child qualifies as under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and none of the disqualifying factors apply.
